evals.expected_output: A single-order execution analysis for the most recent BTC-USD-PERP order. Shows: order details (side, size, order type, limit price if applicable or MARKET), average fill price, arrival price benchmark with slippage in basis points and a rating label (Excellent/Good/Acceptable/Elevated/Poor), period VWAP benchmark with bps delta, a fill detail table, an execution score 1–10 with label, and a 1–2 sentence interpretation.
assertions: Response identifies the order's side (BUY or SELL), size, and order type (LIMIT or MARKET); limit price is shown for limit orders and omitted or marked as MARKET for market orders
Response shows average fill price and a fill detail table listing at minimum: fill time, fill price, fill size, and liquidity type (MAKER or TAKER)
Response includes arrival price benchmark with slippage expressed in basis points and a rating from the defined scale (Excellent/Good/Acceptable/Elevated/Poor)
Response includes period VWAP benchmark with the delta in basis points relative to the fill price
Response shows an execution score between 1 and 10 inclusive, accompanied by a label (Excellent, Good, Fair, or Poor)
Response includes a 1–2 sentence plain-language interpretation of execution quality — it does not merely repeat the numeric values

evals.prompt: show me my order timeline from this morning
evals.expected_output: A chronological order replay for the current UTC morning (roughly 00:00–12:00 UTC or until the current time). Shows a timeline with at least SUBMIT and FILL events for any orders placed, each entry showing timestamp and relevant details. A session summary table covers fill rate, average slippage, maker ratio, and total fees.
assertions: Response includes a chronological timeline where each event shows a timestamp (HH:MM or HH:MM:SS UTC format)
SUBMIT events in the timeline include order type, side, size, and limit price (or MARKET designation)
FILL events in the timeline include fill price, fill size, liquidity type (MAKER/TAKER), and slippage in bps relative to arrival price
CANCEL events are shown in the timeline when applicable, including remaining unfilled size
Response includes a session summary table or equivalent structured block showing at minimum: fill rate, average arrival slippage, maker ratio, and total fees
Session summary includes an overall execution score (1–10 with label) for the morning period

evals.prompt: give me an execution quality report for this week
evals.expected_output: A multi-market execution quality report for the current week. Shows a per-market aggregate table with fill rate, average slippage in bps, VWAP beat/miss in bps, maker percentage, and execution score per market. Includes a patterns detected section and a recommendations section.
assertions: Response includes a per-market aggregate table with at minimum: market name, fill rate (%), average arrival slippage (bps), VWAP beat/miss (bps), maker ratio (%), and score per market
Response includes an overall execution score for the week alongside per-market scores
Response includes a patterns detected section that surfaces at least one behavioral observation (e.g., order type differences, time-of-day effects, or partial fill patterns)
Response includes a recommendations section with at least one actionable suggestion

Slippage sign convention is consistent: positive bps indicates an unfavorable fill (paid above arrival for BUY, sold below arrival for SELL); negative bps indicates a favorable fill; the report does not flip this convention for SELL orders
Response states a specific time window for the report (e.g., week dates or 'Mon–Sun') rather than leaving the covered period vague or unlabeled

evals.prompt: I placed a bunch of market orders — how was my execution?
evals.expected_output: An execution analysis covering recent market orders. Explicitly acknowledges that taker fills are expected and appropriate for market orders — no negative judgment is applied solely for being a taker. Slippage is benchmarked against arrival price. Execution score uses the market-order weight adjustment (fill completeness 40%, VWAP 35%, price walk 25%, maker ratio excluded).
assertions: Response explicitly notes that market orders are expected to fill as taker and does not penalize or flag taker fills as a flaw for market order types
Slippage is benchmarked against arrival price and expressed in basis points — not against a limit price, since market orders have no limit price
Execution score is computed without penalizing the maker ratio component — either the maker ratio factor is excluded or the response notes that it is not applicable for market orders
Fill completeness is weighted heavily (highest or tied-highest factor) in the score methodology for market orders
VWAP comparison is included as a benchmark and weighted significantly for market orders

Execution score between 1 and 10 is shown with a label, and the interpretation reflects market-order context (e.g., comparing slippage against typical taker spread rather than implying maker fill was possible)

trigger_evals.query: how much slippage did I get on my last trade
replay my orders from this morning
find a vault to deposit in
design a momentum strategy for BTC
give me an execution quality report for this week

trigger_evals_validation.query: did I fill at a good price
what was my P&L this week
why did my order fill at that price
how was my maker ratio this week
